tool_args.label: M86 Select Bus Service Progress Report 2017

payload.title: M86 Select Bus Service Progress Report

payload.entity_name: New York City Department of Transportation

payload.description: Connects the dense Manhattan neighborhoods of Upper East Side and Upper West Side
Travels from W 87th Street and West End Avenue on the west to E 92nd Street and York Avenue on the east
Joint DOT and MTA NYCT project to apply BRT principles to the M86 bus route along 86th Street corridor
Select Bus Service conversion of M86 bus route serving 86th Street corridor
payload.limits: W 87th Street and West End Avenue to E 92nd Street and York Avenue

payload.description: Transportation Committee of Manhattan Community Board 7
Transportation Committee of Manhattan Community Board 8
Planning to convert M79 along 79th Street in Manhattan to SBS with tentative launch date of spring 2017

tool_args.label: Pedestrian Safety Neckdowns and Bus Bulbs Capital Project

payload.project_name: Pedestrian Safety Neckdowns and Bus Bulbs Capital Project
payload.description: Addition of six neckdowns and four bus bulbs for pedestrian safety along the M86 SBS route. Construction began June 2016, anticipated completion summer 2017.

Short sections of bus lanes at key intersections to provide bus priority. Westbound queue jump at E 86th Street at 5th Avenue approach. Eastbound queue jumps at W 86th Street and Central Park West entering Transverse Road and exiting at E 84th Street and 5th Avenue.

Customers pay fare before boarding using MetroCard or coin machines at stations, board through any of three doors. All M86 SBS stations except westbound W 86th St & Broadway, eastbound E 91 St & 1 Ave, and Central Park Transverse Road.
Real time passenger information and wayfinding totems at SBS stations with LED screen displaying estimated arrival times. First SBS route to have them at multiple locations during initial launch.

Existing shelters updated with SBS branding. One bench added at westbound E 86 St & Fifth Avenue stop. Shelters to be added at Lexington and Third Avenues as part of bus bulb construction capital project.
Six neckdowns being added for pedestrian safety along the M86 SBS route. Curb line expansion to reduce pedestrian crossing distance.
Four bus bulbs included in M86 SBS project, in both directions at Lexington and 3rd Avenues. Curb extensions at bus stops preventing need for buses to pull in and out of moving lane.
M86 SBS launched after several weeks of SBS station construction and updated road markings installation
Second Avenue Subway Q service began operation with a stop at 86 St
Customer ambassador program covering two shifts (6 am-1 pm and 1 pm-8 pm) over two weeks. Ambassadors also deployed September 8-11 for back-to-school.
M86 SBS Introduction Presentation to Community Board 7
M86 SBS Introduction Presentation to Community Board 8
M86 SBS Implementation Plan Presentation to Community Board 7
M86 SBS Implementation Plan Presentation to Community Board 8

payload.raw_value_text: 96% of riders satisfied with the speed of the ride

payload.context: After SBS implementation, up from 73% before SBS

payload.raw_value_text: 8% decrease in the 5 years preceding SBS implementation
eastbound travel time was reduced 11% from 23.7 minutes to 21.1 minutes
westbound direction, travel times dropped 8% from 22.0 minutes to 20.3 minutes

payload.table_title: Components of Bus Travel Time (in minutes)
Comparison of Time Stopped in Traffic (in minutes)
Percentage of Buses Meeting Wait Assessment Guidelines on M86 and All Manhattan Local Buses, by Time Period, October 2014 vs. October 2016
Before/After Peak Hour Volumes at Intersections with Queue Jump Bus Lanes
rows: EB 86th St at Central Park West
EB 86th St at Central Park West

payload.table_title: Yellow Taxi Monthly Pick-Ups and Drop-Offs on 86th Street, Weekdays, 6 am - 8 pm

payload.raw_value_text: percentage of time spent moving at crawling speed (< 5mph) dropped by an average of 13%
time spent traveling at 15mph+ increased by 14%

payload.context: Comparing time stopped in traffic before and after queue jump lanes

payload.raw_value_text: 30% reduction in the eastbound direction, which had more extensive queue jump treatments
taxi usage dropped 30% from June 2015 (before) to June 2016 (after)
payload.context: Citywide average was 9% decrease for same period

payload.claim_text: The M86 route offers customers a connection to 1 4 5 6 B C trains as well as twelve MTA NYCT bus routes including the M15 SBS.
Prior to SBS, the M86 carried over 24,000 daily passengers, making the M86 the second-busiest crosstown bus route (second to the M14A/D) in Manhattan with the highest per-mile ridership in the city.
The westbound left turn at E 86th Street onto Lexington Avenue was banned in order to improve pedestrian safety at this busy intersection that has an entrance to the 86th Street 4 5 6 subway station.
The M86 SBS layover near the southwest corner of Broadway and W 86th Street has been temporarily relocated to the northeast corner due to building construction.

payload.raw_value_text: Dwell time fell 11% from February 2015 to February 2016 as a result of off-board fare payment
M86 SBS eastbound travel time: 21.1 min
M86 SBS westbound travel time: 20.3 min

payload.claim_text: Community outreach was done primarily through the Transportation Committees of Manhattan Community Boards 7 and 8.

payload.raw_value_text: Ridership grew by an average of 7% in the first 14 months of SBS service
payload.context: First 14 months of SBS service, measured month-to-month compared to 2014/2015

payload.raw_value_text: On W 86th Street at Central Park West, the through movement increased by 13% during the pm peak hour

payload.claim_text: Several intersections have poor safety records resulting in classification as Vision Zero and Safe Routes to School priorities.

The Second Avenue Subway Q service began operation a year and a half after M86 SBS start. It is expected that the Q will have an effect on M86 SBS ridership, and MTA NYCT will monitor possible ridership changes in the coming months.
Analysis of bus speeds found that the M86 was particularly slow entering and leaving Central Park.
As of May 2017, MTA NYCT and NYC DOT have launched 13 Select Bus Service Routes in all five boroughs of New York City.

payload.raw_value_text: citywide average of a 9% decrease for the same time period

payload.claim_text: Ridership measured in August and September 2015, and March and April 2016, was impacted by fare machines at some major stops being out of service due to construction and power outages.
